title: 40Hz Auditory Entrainment
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from elderly dementia patients during 40Hz auditory entrainment sessions designed to investigate gamma-band brain stimulation effects on neural oscillations. Participants underwent either short (6 trials) or long (10 trials) sessions of auditory stimulation using a 5kHz carrier tone amplitude-modulated at 40Hz, with simultaneous 19-channel EEG recording at 250Hz sampling rate. The study examines the effects of auditory gamma-band entrainment on brain oscillations and default mode network connectivity in Alzheimer's disease patients.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded from 19 monopolar channels using the 10/20 system at 250Hz sampling rate with electrode impedance maintained below 20kΩ. Auditory stimulation consisted of a 5kHz carrier tone amplitude-modulated with a 40Hz rectangular wave (4% duty cycle). Each trial lasted 40 seconds followed by 20 seconds of silence. Short sessions included 6 trials while long sessions comprised 10 trials. Preprocessing followed Makoto's pipeline including highpass filtering above 1Hz and line noise removal, with average referencing applied during preprocessing.

funding_references.funder_name: Cognitive Sciences & Technologies Council of Iran
